channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Squishmallows are up to 50% off for October Prime Day
item.description: Squishmallows are as much as 50% off for October Prime Day, which means now is the time to shop for holiday gifts — or even just because.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Trump campaigns in 'make or break' Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Trump barnstormed Pennsylvania on Wednesday as he and his campaign have made clear the commonwealth could make or break his chances of winning in November. Trump held rallies in President Biden’s hometown of Scranton and in Reading.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-Putin ties are back in the spotlight after new book describes calls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new book's assertion that former President Donald Trump may have had as many as seven private phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House has refocused attention on their politically fraught relationship and on Trump's sustained dialogue with w
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jury selection begins in corruption trial of longest-serving legislative leader in US history
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Jury selection began Wednesday in the corruption trial of former Illinois House Speaker Michael Madigan, once the longest-serving legislative leader in American history. The case against Madigan, who resigned in 2021, is one of Illinois’ largest corruption trials in years.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Polluted waste from Florida's fertilizer industry is in the path of Milton's fury
item.description: As Hurricane Milton bears down on Florida’s west coast with powerful winds and flooding rain, environmentalists are worried it could scatter the polluted leftovers of the state’s phosphate fertilizer mining industry and other hazardous waste across the peninsula and into vulnerable waterways.
item.dc:creator: Michael Biesecker And Jason Dearen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Climate solution: Form Energy secures $405M to speed development of long-awaited 100-hour battery
item.description: Form Energy, a company that is beginning to produce a longer-lasting alternative to lithium batteries, hit a milestone Wednesday with an announcement of $405 million in funding.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida trooper rescues dog tied to pole in puddle as Milton heads toward state, DeSantis calls owner 'cruel'
item.description: A dog was left to fend alone amid Hurricane Milton and left tied to a pole by the side of a freeway, Florida authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mozambique counts votes in its presidential election as opposition alleges fraud
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique began counting votes late Wednesday in a presidential election that is expected to extend the ruling party's 49 years in power, though the opposition was already alleging fraud and manipulation.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tampa's 'Lt. Dan' says he's riding out Hurricane Milton on boat despite mayor's warning
item.description: A Tampa man who goes by the nickname "Lt. Dan" has caused a flurry of online concern for his safety during Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina lawmakers pass $273M Helene relief bill with voting changes to more counties
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina legislators completed an initial $273 million relief package on Wednesday to help spur recovery from Hurricane Helene, describing it as a down payment on aid and a way to help hard-hit counties gain more flexibility in holding elections already underway.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina lawmakers pass a $273M Helene relief bill with voting changes to more counties
item.description: North Carolina legislators have approved an initial $273 million relief package to help address Hurricane Helene’s devastation and recovery in the mountains.
item.dc:creator: Gary D. Robertson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Head of international shipping regulator says industry must do more to cut carbon pollution
item.description: HAMBURG, Germany (AP) — For years, the international shipping industry has been criticized for making little progress in reducing the carbon-belching pollution released from the fuels that vessels use in moving most of the cargo that people use every day, such as food, cars and clothing.
item.dc:creator: PETER PRENGAMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: State police recruit's death in Massachusetts overshadows graduation ceremony
item.description: WORCESTER, Mass. (AP) — Holding signs demanding truth and chanting for justice, several dozen people gathered Wednesday outside a graduation ceremony for Massachusetts State Police cadets demanding an explanation of how one of the recruits died during a training exercise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ComEd crews from Chicago area headed to Florida to help with Milton's aftermath
item.description: CHICAGO — Florida residents along the west coast are getting ready for Hurricane Milton, as local ComEd crews are headed down to the state to help with the storm's aftermath.
item.dc:creator: Brónagh Tumulty, WGN 9

item.title: 5 dead after a small plane crashes on Santa Catalina Island off the Southern California coast
item.description: AVALON, Calif. (AP) — Five people were killed when a small plane crashed during takeoff from Santa Catalina Island off the Southern California coast, authorities said Wednesday. The twin-engine Beechcraft 95 crashed shortly after 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Volunteers at Concordia Cemetery use technology to unearth Buffalo history
item.description: "We're going to be looking for and bringing up some of the tombstones that have sunk or have been buried," said Bonnie Fleischauer, Concordia Cemetery's public information officer.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: Language, lack of money and fear are matters of life and death with Milton approaching Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton is expected to unleash its greatest force over hundreds of thousands of immigrants who don’t speak English.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE NOW: Pres. Biden delivers remarks as Hurricane Milton closes in on Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton has weakened to a Category 3 storm ahead of landfall but remains a "grave threat," forecasters warn.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Honda recalls about 1.7 million vehicles over steering issue
item.description: Honda is recalling nearly 1.7 million vehicles in the U.S. over a steering issue that could result in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: LOOK: Tornado crosses Florida interstate as Hurricane Milton churns closer
item.description: As Florida's Gulf Coast braces for the impacts of Hurricane Milton Wednesday, the storm is causing severe weather across the state.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, Fox 5

item.title: Feeling stressed about the election? Here's what some are doing and what they say you can do too
item.description: If the high stakes presidential election is causing troublesome thoughts, existential dread or rifts with loved ones, there’s no need to white knuckle through it. Take a deep breath. Literally.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida Republican suggests Greene have ‘head examined’ over hurricane claims
item.description: Florida Republican Rep. Carlos A. Gimenez suggested Wednesday that Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) should have her “head examined” after she claimed humans can control the weather.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: NC National Guard helicopter crew grounded after 'rotor wash' incident blows away Helene aid
item.description: Video of the incident appears to show the wind from the helicopter blowing over tents and scattering items in the air.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, KRON4

item.title: Climate change boosted Helene's deadly rain and wind and the same is likely for Milton: Scientists
item.description: Human-caused climate change boosted a devastating Hurricane Helene 's rainfall by about 10% and intensified its winds by about 11%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Harris aims to help adult caregivers with Medicare benefit expansion
item.description: Vice President Harris on Tuesday introduced a new plan to expand Medicare benefits so that the program covers the costs of long-term home care for older Americans. The plan, formally announced on ABC’s “The View,” is aimed at helping the “sandwich generation” take care of their aging parents.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Chipotle announces the return of its ‘Boorito’ promotion
item.description: Chipotle is celebrating the spookiest of holidays with the return of its popular “Boo-rito” promotion. During the promotion, rewards members can purchase entrees for $6 and enjoy extended store hours. Only rewards members who order in-store can take advantage of the promo.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Hurricane hunters take turbulent trip through Hurricane Milton
item.description: Researchers on a Hurricane Hunter aircraft had a bump ride as they flew into Hurricane Milton on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: This Montana Senate candidate said his opponent ate 'lobbyist steak.' But he lobbied—with steak
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — During an October debate, Montana GOP Senate candidate Tim Sheehy denounced his Democratic opponent, Sen. Jon Tester, for ” eating lobbyist steak ″ while the Republican fought in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: An unusual hurricane season goes from ultra quiet to record busy and spawns Helene and Milton
item.description: Explosively intensifying Hurricane Milton is the latest freaky system to come out of what veteran hurricane scientists call the weirdest storm season of their lives.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security COLA for 2025 predicted to be lower than 2024
item.description: The Social Security Administration will announce a new cost-of-living adjustment on Oct. 10, 2024, with the increase expected to be smaller than last year.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9
